title: The Clockmaker's Asylum
setting: Blackwood Chronos Asylum — a Victorian clock tower converted into an abandoned asylum, where stopped time has trapped clockwork patients in frozen horror.

characters.name: Dr. Silas Blackwood
characters.description: The asylum's founder. His portrait hangs in the foyer, eyes unnervingly lifelike.

characters.name: The Warden
characters.description: A hollow-eyed figure in tattered robes, standing perfectly still in the clock-chamber.

items.name: Winding Key
items.description: A tarnished brass key with gear-shaped teeth. Warm to the touch, as if it remembers turning.

items.name: Brass Lever
items.description: A short, heavy lever with a worn grip. Stained with oil and something darker.

items.name: Gear Component
items.description: A small brass gear, caked with decades of grime and old blood.

items.name: Oil-Soaked Rag
items.description: A filthy rag, stiff with congealed oil. It smells of machine rooms and neglect.

items.name: Cleaned Gear
items.description: The gear component, now gleaming. Its teeth are perfectly cut, precise enough to open anything.

items.name: Master Winding Key
items.description: A large, ornate key of blackened steel. The clockmaker's master tool — it controls everything.

scenes.name: The Foyer
scenes.prose:
The asylum's entrance hall smells of dust and stopped time. A massive grandfather clock dominates the far wall, its face frozen at 11:59. To the east, a door stands locked with a brass mechanism. Dr. Blackwood's portrait watches from the stairs, his painted eyes seeming to track your movement.

In the corner, a winding key rests atop a side table, half-buried in fallen plaster.

lookDescriptions.grandfather-clock: The clock's pendulum hangs motionless. Behind its glass face, tiny gears have rusted into a single seized mass. The minute and hour hands are locked at 11:59.
lookDescriptions.portrait: Dr. Silas Blackwood, stern and unblinking. The paint has cracked around his mouth, giving him a permanent, terrible smile.
lookDescriptions.locked-door: A heavy oak door reinforced with brass fittings. A keyhole in the center is shaped like a gear, waiting for the right key.

scenes.name: The Workshop
scenes.prose:
Gears litter every surface — brass, iron, some small enough for a pocket watch, others large as cartwheels. A workbench stands against the north wall, dominated by a rusted gear-valve. Steam pipes run along the ceiling, silent and cold. Something rolls beneath the bench: a small gear component, pushed there by the last occupant who never left.

To the north, steel stairs lead upward, but the mechanism is sealed by a lever-slot in the wall.

lookDescriptions.gear-valve: A large brass valve, seized with rust. It has a slot designed to accept a lever. Oil stains drip from its base like old blood.
lookDescriptions.workbench: Tools lie scattered in disarray — screwdrivers, pliers, a magnifying glass. Everything is coated in a fine layer of brass dust.
lookDescriptions.steel-stairs: Narrow metal stairs ascending through darkness. At the top, a door is held shut by a hydraulic lock. The lever-slot beside it is empty.

scenes.name: The Patient Ward
scenes.prose:
Rows of iron beds stretch into shadow, their restraints still fastened. At the far end, a door leads west to the clock-chamber — locked by a gear mechanism that requires a specific component. The wall is covered in frantic scratches: tally marks, a hundred of them, ending abruptly.

To the south, stairs descend into darkness. You can smell oil and mildew rising from below.

lookDescriptions.iron-beds: The restraints are buckled tight. One bed has a small metal plate bolted to its frame — a slot shaped like a gear. Something could fit there.
lookDescriptions.scratches: Tally marks, carved deep into the plaster. Someone counted the days. Or the ticks. They stopped at one hundred.
lookDescriptions.gear-lock: A circular mechanism built into the door. It has grooves designed to accept a cleaned gear component. Without the right piece, the door will not open.

scenes.name: The Basement
scenes.prose:
The air is thick with the smell of stale oil and copper. A single bare bulb flickers overhead, its filament dying. Against one wall, a stack of oil-soaked rags crumbles to dust at your touch. The floor is slick with dark fluid — old lubricant, or something else.

There is nothing else here but the rags and the darkness. The stairs back up are your only escape.

lookDescriptions.oil-rags: A pile of filthy rags, stiff with dried oil. One rag seems less degraded than the others — it could be used to clean something heavily grimed.
lookDescriptions.bare-bulb: A single light bulb hangs from a fraying wire. It flickers irregularly, as if something in the walls is draining the power.
lookDescriptions.dark-fluid: Puddles of dark liquid coat the floor. It smells of machine oil and something sweetly organic. You do not want to touch it.

scenes.name: The Clock Chamber
scenes.prose:
The heart of the tower. Gears massive as doors line the walls, connected by chains and pistons that hang in frozen motion. In the center stands the Warden — a figure in tattered asylum robes, perfectly still, its head tilted at an impossible angle. Behind it, a clockwork panel bears a gear-shaped slot.

Above, a ladder leads to the belfry. It is locked by the clock's mechanism. When the right gear is inserted, the inscription 'XII - III - IX' will be revealed.

lookDescriptions.clockwork-panel: A brass panel with a gear-shaped slot. When a cleaned gear is inserted, a hidden compartment will open, revealing the master winding key and a cryptic code.
lookDescriptions.the-warden: It does not move. It does not breathe. Its eyes are glass, but they follow you. The Warden has been waiting for the clock to restart.
lookDescriptions.ladder: An iron ladder ascending through a hatch in the ceiling. A locking mechanism is engaged — the clock's code must be solved before it will release.

scenes.name: The Belfry
scenes.prose:
The great bell hangs above, silent and massive. Below it, the main clockface dominates the wall — three rotatable hands set to random positions. The code 'XII - III - IX' is etched into the brass rim. In your pocket, the master winding key grows warm.

Insert the key into the clockface. Set the hands to the correct positions. Time has waited long enough.

lookDescriptions.main-clockface: Three rotatable hands on a vast brass face. The code XII-III-IX is etched below. Setting the hands correctly will restart the clock and break the asylum's curse.
lookDescriptions.the-bell: A bell the size of a carriage. It has not chimed in decades. When time restarts, it will toll for the first time since the asylum fell.
lookDescriptions.master-key-slot: A keyhole at the center of the clockface, shaped for the master winding key. The key fits perfectly. Turning it is only the first step.

puzzles.result: The great clock lurches forward. Gears grind behind the walls. A hidden panel clicks open in the clock's base, and the brass lever falls free.
Steam hisses from a burst pipe. The valve shrieks open, flooding the workbench with oil. A small gear component rolls free from the machinery.
You clean the grime from the gear. Its teeth are perfectly shaped. It slots into the ward's door-lock mechanism with a satisfying click.
The gear slides home. The panel opens, revealing the master winding key and an inscription: 'XII - III - IX'. The ladder lock disengages.

puzzles.action: use master-winding-key on main-clockface then set hands to XII-III-IX
puzzles.result: The key turns. The hands align. The bell tolls — once, twice, twelve times. Time restarts. The clockwork patients freeze. You are free.

winCondition.description: Set the clock hands to XII-III-IX using the master winding key to restart time and break the asylum's curse.
